content: Alcohol is converted to acetaldehyde in the mouth by enzymes, which is highly toxic.
The liver converts acetaldehyde into acetate, but it can only do so quickly, leading to a buildup of acetaldehyde in the system.
Acetaldehyde affects kidney function, causing dehydration and a hangover-like effect due to reduced water reabsorption.
Drinking distilled alcohol, such as vodka, reduces the liver's workload by removing impurities and making it easier for the liver to metabolize the alcohol.
Limiting alcohol intake to one type per night helps the liver focus on metabolizing one substance at a time, reducing confusion and improving overall liver function.
Eating foods rich in healthy fats, such as eggs and bacon, can help replenish brain fats and improve cognitive function after a night of drinking.
